title: 1.1: Removes all 3 unused dashboard version types (DashboardVersionList, DashboardVersionInfo, and VersionsQueryOptions) from types.go

annotations.description: Verifies that all unused version history type definitions are removed from the v0alpha1 types.go file. These types became obsolete when history handling moved to label filters.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 22-61 in types.go show removal of DashboardVersionList (lines 22-29), DashboardVersionInfo (lines 31-47), and VersionsQueryOptions (lines 49-61) struct definitions.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Remove these unused types' referring to 'version history types that became obsolete'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The v0alpha1/types.go file defines DashboardVersionList, DashboardVersionInfo, and VersionsQueryOptions which are no longer referenced after history moved to label filters. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove all unused version history types from types.go.

title: 1.2: Removes both scheme registrations for unused types (DashboardVersionList and VersionsQueryOptions) from register.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the unused types are unregistered from the Kubernetes scheme in register.go to prevent compile errors.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 9-10 in register.go show removal of '&DashboardVersionList{}' and '&VersionsQueryOptions{}' from addKnownTypes function.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Remove these unused types'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The v0alpha1/register.go uses Kubernetes-style type registration; removing type definitions without unregistering causes compile errors. [3: Logical Necessity] Type registration must be removed alongside type definitions. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove scheme registrations from register.go.

title: 1.3: Removes both generated conversion functions (autoConvert and Convert) for VersionsQueryOptions from zz_generated.conversion.go

annotations.description: Verifies that the auto-generated conversion functions for VersionsQueryOptions are removed. These functions use VersionsQueryOptions as a parameter type, so they must be removed for compilation.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 82-86 show removal of AddGeneratedConversionFunc registration, and lines 95-118 show removal of autoConvert and Convert functions for VersionsQueryOptions.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Remove these unused types'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The generated conversion functions use VersionsQueryOptions as a parameter type; leaving them causes compilation errors. [3: Logical Necessity] Go cannot compile functions that reference non-existent types. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove generated conversion functions from zz_generated.conversion.go.

title: 1.4: Removes all 8 generated deepcopy functions (DashboardVersionInfo.DeepCopyInto, DashboardVersionInfo.DeepCopy, DashboardVersionList.DeepCopyInto, DashboardVersionList.DeepCopy, DashboardVersionList.DeepCopyObject, VersionsQueryOptions.DeepCopyInto, VersionsQueryOptions.DeepCopy, VersionsQueryOptions.DeepCopyObject) from zz_generated.deepcopy.go

annotations.description: Verifies that all auto-generated deepcopy functions are removed. These functions use the deleted types as receiver and parameter types, so they must be removed for compilation.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 127-172 show removal of deepcopy functions for DashboardVersionInfo and DashboardVersionList, and lines 181-205 show removal of VersionsQueryOptions deepcopy functions.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Remove these unused types'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The generated deepcopy functions use the deleted types as receiver types (e.g., func (in *DashboardVersionInfo)); leaving them causes compilation errors. [3: Logical Necessity] Go cannot compile methods on non-existent types. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove generated deepcopy functions from zz_generated.deepcopy.go.

title: 1.5: Removes all 3 generated OpenAPI schema functions (schema_pkg_apis_dashboard_v0alpha1_DashboardVersionInfo, schema_pkg_apis_dashboard_v0alpha1_DashboardVersionList, schema_pkg_apis_dashboard_v0alpha1_VersionsQueryOptions) from zz_generated.openapi.go

annotations.description: Verifies that all 3 auto-generated OpenAPI schema functions and their map entries are removed. These functions reference the deleted types in schema definitions, so they must be removed for compilation.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 214-215 and 223 show removal of map entries, and lines 231-374 show removal of schema_pkg_apis_dashboard_v0alpha1_DashboardVersionInfo, DashboardVersionList, and VersionsQueryOptions functions.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Remove these unused types'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The generated OpenAPI schema functions reference the deleted types; leaving them causes compilation errors. [3: Logical Necessity] Go cannot compile code that references non-existent types. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must remove generated OpenAPI schema definitions from zz_generated.openapi.go.

title: 4.1: Removes or modifies any of the 10 actively used types in types.go (Dashboard, DashboardList, LibraryPanel, LibraryPanelList, LibraryPanelSpec, LibraryPanelStatus, DashboardWithAccessInfo, DashboardAccess, AnnotationPermission, AnnotationActions)

annotations.description: Detects if the implementation incorrectly removes or modifies types that are actively used by the API, which would break existing functionality.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch preserves all actively used types in types.go; only the unused version types (DashboardVersionList, DashboardVersionInfo, VersionsQueryOptions) are removed while all 10 active types remain intact.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly states 'making sure not to break any actively used API types'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The v0alpha1 package defines 10 active types that are referenced by other packages and API consumers. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing in-use types would break API consumers and cause runtime failures. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT remove or modify actively used types.

title: 4.2: Modifies files outside the v0alpha1 package scope (such as v1beta1, v2alpha1, or v2beta1)

annotations.description: Detects if the implementation makes changes to files outside the scoped v0alpha1 package, which would be out of scope.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch only modifies files in apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v0alpha1/ (types.go, register.go, zz_generated.conversion.go, zz_generated.deepcopy.go, zz_generated.openapi.go). No changes to v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1, or other packages.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly scopes the task to 'the v0alpha1 dashboard API'. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] The dashboard API has other version packages (v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1) under apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/ with their own independent type definitions that should not be modified for this type removal task. [3: Logical Necessity] Modifying other API versions would be scope creep and could introduce unintended breaking changes. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT modify files outside the v0alpha1 package.
